name: BibleDesk
tagline: AI Bible study with original language depth — free for everyone
description: BibleDesk is a free AI-powered Bible study platform that goes deeper than surface-level answers. Ask any question about Scripture and get a structured response across five dimensions: the text itself, its historical context, the original Hebrew or Greek meaning, theological significance, and practical application for today.
forWho: Anyone who takes their faith seriously and wants to study Scripture more deeply — without needing a seminary degree. Pastors, small group leaders, and personal study. Built free and intended to stay that way.
story: Faith is a big part of who I am. I wanted a tool that could help me study the Bible the way I actually wanted to — not just a search engine for verses, but something that could help me understand what a word meant in Koine Greek, what was happening historically when Paul wrote a letter, and what it means for how I live today. I built BibleDesk for myself first, and made it free because this isn't the kind of tool that should have a paywall.
details: React frontend, Supabase for user sessions and saved studies. AI responses structured as five-dimension answers: Scripture analysis, historical context, original language (Hebrew/Greek), theological meaning, and practical application. No API key required for users — completely free access.
